question: I want to study the demographic in NYC. Can you find me the zip code of the NYC high school that was in the top 3 by overall progress score in the school year 2011-13 and had an A grade in the 2010-11 progress. Report the percentage of White people contributing to the racial makeup of the neighborhood whose primary ZIP code is the same as that high school based on data in 2010 census?. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact:
import os
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3

source = "datagov/2011-2012-high-school-progress-report/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
local_path = source if os.path.exists(source) else "/tmp/k123_data_cache/" + source

if os.path.exists(local_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(local_path, sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
else:
    obj = boto3.client("s3").get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
    df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
import re

school_col = "School Name"
overall_col = "Overall Score"

filtered = df.copy()
if "School Type" in filtered.columns:
    filtered = filtered[filtered["School Type"].astype(str).str.contains("High", case=False, na=False)]

filtered[overall_col] = pd.to_numeric(filtered[overall_col], errors="coerce")
filtered = filtered.sort_values(overall_col, ascending=False)

def normalize_school(name):
    s = str(name)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+at\s+.+$", "", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\bP\.?\s*S\.?\s*0*", "PS ", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s).strip()
    return s

answer = filtered[school_col].dropna().astype(str).head(3).map(normalize_school).tolist()
1.subquestion: Which NYC high schools were in the top 3 by overall score in 2011-12?
answer: Academy for Careers in Television and Film

3.fact:
import os
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3

source = "datagov/2013-2014-doe-high-school-performance-directory/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
local_path = source if os.path.exists(source) else "/tmp/k123_data_cache/" + source

if os.path.exists(local_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(local_path, sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
else:
    obj = boto3.client("s3").get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
    df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
node_1_answer = ['It Takes a Village Academy', 'Academy for Careers in Television and Film', 'Manhattan Village Academy']
node_2_answer = ['It Takes a Village Academy', 'Academy for Careers in Television and Film', 'Brooklyn International High School']

import os
import io
import re

def normalize_school(name):
    s = str(name)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+at\s+.+$", "", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\bP\.?\s*S\.?\s*0*", "PS ", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s).strip()
    return s.lower()

candidate_names = sorted(set(node_1_answer) & set(node_2_answer))
candidate_norm_to_name = {normalize_school(x): str(x).strip() for x in candidate_names}

lookup_source = "datagov/2012-2013-high-school-progress-report/files/rows.txt"
lookup_local = lookup_source if os.path.exists(lookup_source) else "/tmp/k123_data_cache/" + lookup_source
if os.path.exists(lookup_local):
    lookup_df = pd.read_csv(lookup_local, sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
else:
    obj = boto3.client("s3").get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=lookup_source)
    lookup_df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)

lookup_df["_norm_name"] = lookup_df["School Name"].astype(str).map(normalize_school)
name_to_dbn = {
    row["_norm_name"]: str(row["DBN"]).strip()
    for _, row in lookup_df.iterrows()
    if row["_norm_name"] in candidate_norm_to_name
}

candidate_dbns = set(name_to_dbn.values())
filtered = df[
    df["DBN"].astype(str).str.strip().isin(candidate_dbns)
    & df["Progress_Rpt_10-11"].astype(str).str.strip().str.upper().eq("A")
]

dbns = filtered["DBN"].astype(str).str.strip().tolist()
answer = [candidate_norm_to_name[norm_name] for norm_name, dbn in name_to_dbn.items() if dbn in dbns]
3.subquestion: Which schools from <intersection of node_1, node_2> had an A grade in the 2010-11 Progress Report?

4.fact:
import os
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3

source = "datagov/2012-2013-school-locations/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
local_path = source if os.path.exists(source) else "/tmp/k123_data_cache/" + source

if os.path.exists(local_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(local_path, sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
else:
    obj = boto3.client("s3").get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
    df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
node_3_answer = ['It Takes a Village Academy']

import re

def normalize_school(name):
    s = str(name)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+at\s+.+$", "", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\bP\.?\s*S\.?\s*0*", "PS ", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s).strip()
    return s.lower()

target = node_3_answer[0] if isinstance(node_3_answer, list) and node_3_answer else node_3_answer
target_norm = normalize_school(target)

df["_norm_loc"] = df["LOCATION_NAME"].astype(str).map(normalize_school)
row = df[df["_norm_loc"].eq(target_norm)].iloc[0]
loc_text = str(row.get("Location 1", ""))
match = re.search(r"\b(\d{5})(?:-\d{4})?\b", loc_text)
if match:
    answer = match.group(1)
else:
    addr_text = str(row.get("PRIMARY_ADDRESS_LINE_1", ""))
    match2 = re.search(r"\b(\d{5})(?:-\d{4})?\b", addr_text)
    answer = match2.group(1) if match2 else ""
answer = str(answer)
4.subquestion: What is the ZIP code of <node_3 answer>?

5.fact: The racial makeup of the neighborhood was 1.3% (1,816) White, 88.7% (120,231) African American, 0.3% (366) Native American, 1.1% (1,480) Asian, 0% (45) Pacific Islander, 0.4% (523) from other races, and 1.6% (2,140) from two or more races based on 2010 census.
5.subquestion: What percentage does White people contribute to the racial makeup of the neighborhood whose primary ZIP code is <node_4 answer> based on data in 2010 census?

reasoning_chain: HOP 1 (d=2, NYC high school top-3 overall score - nodes 1-2):
Node 1 (2011-12): Top 3 -> {It Takes a Village Academy, Academy for Careers in Television and Film, Manhattan Village Academy}
Node 2 (2012-13): Top 3 -> {It Takes a Village Academy, Academy for Careers in Television and Film, Brooklyn International High School}
Intersection(1,2) -> {It Takes a Village Academy, Academy for Careers in Television and Film}
HOP 2 (d=1, school grades - node 3 depends on hop 1):
Node 3: From the intersection, the school with an A grade in 2010-11 -> It Takes a Village Academy
HOP 3 (d=1, school locations - node 4 depends on node 3):
Node 4: ZIP code of It Takes a Village Academy -> 11203
HOP 4 (d=1, neighborhood demographics - node 5 depends on node 4):
Node 5: White population share for the neighborhood with primary ZIP 11203 -> 1.3%

Node 2 (2012-13): Top 3 -> {It Takes a Village Academy, Academy for Careers in Television and Film, Brooklyn International High School}. Intersection(1,2) -> {It Takes a Village Academy, Academy for Careers in Television and Film}.
